title: Drug-induced cardiac toxicity and adverse drug reactions, a narrative review.
claims: Drug-induced cardiotoxicity is a significant concern in drug development and clinical practice, affecting both cardiovascular and non-cardiovascular drugs.

Cardiac adverse drug reactions (ADRs) can lead to severe consequences such as heart failure, myocardial ischemia, valvular disease, thrombosis, myocarditis, pericarditis, arrhythmias, and conduction abnormalities.

Multi-stage evaluation guidelines based on ICH standards are used for in vitro and in vivo testing of cardiotoxicity but post-marketing surveillance is still critical due to potential escape from initial scrutiny.
